The unique identifier for the equipment, which should follow the BIC ISO Container Identification Number where possible.
According to [ISO 6346](https://www.iso.org/standard/83558.html), a container identification code consists of a 4-letter prefix and a 7-digit number (composed of a 3-letter owner code, a category identifier, a serial number, and a check-digit).

If a container does not comply with [ISO 6346](https://www.iso.org/standard/83558.html), it is suggested to follow [Recommendation #2: Containers with non-ISO identification](https://smdg.org/documents/smdg-recommendations) from SMDG.
Packaging information for this cargo item.
A list of `Dangerous Goods`

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / additionalContainerCargoHandling
Example: To be handled with extreme care
Description: Text field to provide cargo handling information already known at the booking stage.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / additionalRegulatoryStatements
Description:
Additional mandatory regulatory wording required by IMDG special provisions, class rules, or competent authority approvals.
Item description:
The regulatory statement applicable to the dangerous goods cargo.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / codedVariantList
Description:
Four-character code supplied by Exis Technologies that assists to remove ambiguities when identifying a variant within a single UN number or NA number that may occur when two companies exchange DG information.

Character | Valid Characters | Description
:--------:|------------------|------------
1| 0, 1, 2, 3|The packing group. Code 0 indicates there is no packing group
2|0 to 9 and A to Z|A sequence letter for the PSN, or 0 if there were no alternative PSNs
3 and 4|0 to 9 and A to Z|Two sequence letters for other information, for the cases where the variant is required because of different in subrisks, packing instruction etc.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / competentAuthorityApproval
Description: Name and reference number of the competent authority providing the approval.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / dateOfPackingIntoPackagings
Description: Date on which the material was packed into the packaging. Required for UN 1361.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / dateOfProduction
Description: Production date of the material. Required for UN 1361.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / emergencyContactDetails
Description: 24 hr emergency contact details

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / emergencyContactDetails / contact
Description: Name of the contact person during an emergency.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / emergencyContactDetails / phone
Description: Phone number for the contact, including an international phone number format as defined in the [ITU-T recommendation E.123](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.123/en).

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / emergencyContactDetails / provider
Description: Name of the third party vendor providing emergency support

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / emergencyContactDetails / referenceNumber
Description: Contract reference for the emergency support provided by an external third party vendor.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / EMSNumber
Description: The emergency schedule identified in the IMO EmS Guide — Emergency Response Procedures for Ships Carrying Dangerous Goods. Comprises 2 values; 1 for spillage and 1 for fire. Possible values spillage: S-A to S-Z. Possible values fire: F-A to F-Z.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / endOfHoldingTime
Description: Date by when the refrigerated liquid needs to be delivered.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / fireworkClassificationReferences
Description:
Classification reference(s) issued by the competent authority for fireworks transported under UN Nos. 0333, 0334, 0335, 0336, and 0337.
Item description:
The classification reference(s) shall consist of the competent authority's state, indicated by the distinguishing sign used on vehicles in international road traffic, the competent authority identification and a unique serial reference.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / fumigationDateTime
Description: Date & time when the container was fumigated

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / grossWeight
Description: Total weight of the goods carried, including packaging.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / grossWeight / unit
Description:
Measurement unit in which the weight `value` is expressed:
- `KGM` (Kilograms)
- `LBR` (Pounds)
- `GRM` (Grams)
- `ONZ` (Ounce)

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / grossWeight / value
Description: Weight value expressed in the measurement `unit`

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / IMOClass
Description:
The hazard class code of the referenced dangerous goods according to the specified regulation. Examples of possible values are:

- `1.1A` (Substances and articles which have a mass explosion hazard)
- `1.6N` (Extremely insensitive articles which do not have a mass explosion hazard)
- `2.1` (Flammable gases)
- `8` (Corrosive substances)

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / inhalationZone
Description:
The zone classification of the toxicity of the inhalant. Possible values are:

- `A` (Hazard Zone A) can be assigned to specific gases and liquids
- `B` (Hazard Zone B) can be assigned to specific gases and liquids
- `C` (Hazard Zone C) can **only** be assigned to specific gases
- `D` (Hazard Zone D) can **only** be assigned to specific gases

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / innerPackagings
Description: A list of `Inner Packings` contained inside this `outer packaging/overpack`.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / innerPackagings / description
Description: Description of the packaging.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / innerPackagings / material
Description: The `material` used for the `Inner Packaging` of the referenced `Dangerous Goods`.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / innerPackagings / quantity
Description: Count of `Inner Packagings` of the referenced `Dangerous Goods`.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isCompetentAuthorityApprovalRequired
Description: Indicates if the cargo require approval from authorities

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isEmptyUncleanedResidue
Description: Indicates if the cargo is residue.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isExceptedQuantity
Description: Indicates if the dangerous goods can be transported as excepted quantity in accordance with Chapter 3.5 of the IMO IMDG Code.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isHot
Description: Indicates if high temperature cargo is shipped.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isLimitedQuantity
Description: Indicates if the dangerous goods can be transported as limited quantity in accordance with Chapter 3.4 of the IMO IMDG Code.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isMarinePollutant
Description: Indicates if the goods belong to the classification of Marine Pollutant.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isMolten
Description: Indicates that the dangerous goods are offered for transport in molten state. Required when applicable unless already included in the properShippingName.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isReportableQuantity
Description: Indicates if a container of hazardous material is at the reportable quantity level. If `true`, a report to the relevant authority must be made in case of spill.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isSalvagePackings
Description: Indicates if the cargo has special packaging for the transport, recovery or disposal of damaged, defective, leaking or nonconforming hazardous materials packages, or hazardous materials that have spilled or leaked.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isStabilizedSubstance
Description: Indicates that the substance is stabilized for transport purposes. Required when applicable unless already included in the properShippingName.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isTemperatureControlled
Description: Indicates that stabilization is achieved through temperature control during transport. Required when applicable unless already included in the properShippingName.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / isWaste
Description: Indicates if waste is being shipped

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / limits
Description: Limits for the `Dangerous Goods`. The same `Temperature Unit` must apply to all attributes in this structure.

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / limits / flashPoint

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / limits / SADT

Path: DangerousGoodsDeclaration / consignmentItems / cargoItems / outerPackaging / dangerousGoods / limits / SAPT

Description: Temperature at which emergency procedures shall be implemented

Item description:
Grouping of Dangerous Goods having certain similar chemical properties. Possible values are:

- `2` (Ammonium Compounds)
- `3` (Bromates)

- `15` (Powdered metals)
- `16` (Peroxides),
- `17` (Azides)
- `18` (Alkalis)

According to IMDG Code 5.4.1.6.2: "If the dangerous goods documentation is presented to the carrier by means of EDP or EDI transmission techniques, the signature(s) may be electronic signature(s) or may be replaced by the name(s) (in capitals) of the person authorized to sign."

Free-format identifier used to specify a certain type of DG Declaration, for example "English" or "French".

A declaration with the same `declarationReference` and with a newer `declarationDateTime` only overwrite a declaration with the same `declarationReference` and an older `declarationDateTime` if the two declarations also have the same `typeLabel` (or if both have no `typeLabel`).
The equipments being used.
The container or transport equipment used.

The unique identifier for the equipment, which should follow the BIC ISO Container Identification Number where possible. According to ISO 6346, a container identification code consists of a 4-letter prefix and a 7-digit number (composed of a 3-letter owner code, a category identifier, a serial number, and a check-digit).

If a container does not comply with ISO 6346, it is suggested to follow Recommendation #2: Containers with non-ISO identification from SMDG.

Unique code for the different equipment size and type used to transport commodities. The code can refer to one of ISO size type (e.g. 22G1) or ISO type group (e.g. 22GP) following the ISO 6346 standard.
The tare weight of the container

- `GRM` (Grams)
- `ONZ` (Ounce)

Export voyage number or reference
Carrier-specific identifier of the voyage

Unique identifier of the voyage within a service, assigned by carriers as specified by DCSA.

Carriers assign a Universal Voyage Reference (UVR) to a voyage,
either individually for non-VSA services or jointly for shared services under a VSA or Alliance.

Every Universal Voyage Reference is a 5-character string with the following format:
 * the last 2 digits of the year
 * 2 alphanumeric characters for the sequence number of the voyage
 * 1 character for the initial of the direction/haul (`N`orth, `E`ast, `W`est, `S`outh or `R`oundtrip).
Import voyage number or reference
